articles.title: MINUTE READ)
articles.summary: AI's potential is often exaggerated. It likely won't revolutionize every product. Nevertheless, AI stands to revolutionize analytics and performance measurement products, enhancing user experience by providing precise, contextual insights for better business decisions.

articles.title: WALMART, AND STARTUPS
articles.summary: A designer shares the most vital learnings from their 10-year career: designers need to have an iterative mindset, build relationships, think long-term and beyond the MVP, stay curious and ask dumb questions, and continue to learn and embrace new technologies.

articles.title: STATE OF CSS: 2023
articles.summary: This year's edition of the State of CSS is out! Based on a survey with 9,190 developers worldwide, it decodes a hectic year. Understand the most used tools, features, frameworks, and much more.

articles.title: HOW STORYTELLING TURNS DIGITAL DESIGNS INTO EMOTION
articles.summary: In the realm of product design, the fusion of storytelling and technology creates captivating user experiences. Narratives emotionally connect users with products, as seen in Spotify's music journey or Airbnb's accommodation narratives. This approach transforms interactions, enhances user experiences, and establishes strong brand identities.

articles.title: MINUTE READ)
articles.summary: This article discusses the use of variables in Figma for the color system and modes in the design system starter kit. Discover how to use Figma variables to establish a color system and modes and gain insights on how semantic color variables can enhance design clarity, readability, and the creation of balanced color palettes for different modes.

articles.title: TACTILE INTO DESIGNING WITH GENERATIVE AI
articles.summary: Is prompting still far away from mimicking human conversation? Adding tactile controls to the equation might be the missing element. Proposed for the Royal College of Art Graduate Show in London, this new interface one day might allow designers to physically manipulate textures and materials using the power of AI.

articles.title: WEB UX: STUDY GUIDE
articles.summary: An exhaustive resource for insights on how users interact with the web and how to design better web user experiences. It includes guides for viewing behaviors, link usability, navigation, searching, web design trends, and web e-commerce.

articles.title: COLOR PALETTES FROM THE WORLD'S GREATEST ARTISTS
articles.summary: Of course, they've also mastered colors! Check out the color palettes used in famous paintings by the best artists of all time.

articles.title: 250+ STUNNING FLAG ICONS
articles.summary: This open-source flag icon set is all you need to become the ultimate cosmopolitan designer. Download the optimized version for Sketch or Figma. Or install and use with just a few lines of code. Available for React, Vue, and Svelte.

articles.title: HOW DUOLINGO REIGNITED USER GROWTH
articles.summary: Jorge Mazal, former CPO of Duolingo, tells the story behind a 4.5x growth. For a mature product, that's a lot. Learn about the app's innovative growth model and how small changes led to massive outcomes.

articles.title: WHEN TO AVOID ONBOARDING
articles.summary: Most onboarding flows are about confirmation windows and blocking messages. That's bad design. Users will learn the best when the context is appropriate. In that sense, onboarding could prevent you from identifying where and when to communicate with users.

articles.title: EUROPEAN ACCESSIBILITY ACT: WHAT YOU NEED TO KNOW
articles.summary: The deadline is set for 2025, and the proposed changes will considerably impact websites and applications in Europe. If your company sells to EU customers, it must comply with the new law.

articles.title: BROWSE WEBSITES BY FONTS
articles.summary: A gallery of websites categorized by the fonts they use. Also comes with common font pairings and alternatives.

articles.title: HYPER-MODERNIST MOVIE POSTERS
articles.summary: A circle overlaid by two diagonals, all inscribed in a square. Nothing else. Period. And still, we bet you'll be able to recognize some redesigned movie posters.

articles.title: ADAPTING ILLUSTRATIONS TO DARK MODE
articles.summary: CSS can help adjust images and illustrations on a website when switched to dark mode. Two things are key: inverting the background color but preserving the hues of the other colors. The invert function from CSS filter effects, combined with the hue-rotate function does the trick.
